entries.response:
Here is a sample plant UML diagram generated using the `plant` library:
```
# Plant UML Diagram
+---------------+
|  Plant Species  |  Function      |
+---------------+
|  A (A-V, A-I)          |  B (B-V, B-I)          |
|  C (C-V, C-I)          |  D (D-V, D-I)          |
+---------------+
|      E-V (E-V, E-V)          |  F (F-V, F-V)          |
+---------------+
|    A(A-V, A-I, B-V, C-V)           |    B(B-V, B-I, C-V, D-V)            |
|    A(A-V, A-I, B-V, C-V)           |    D(D-V, D-I, B-V, E-V)             |
+---------------+
```
Here is a sample of the diagram:
```
# Plant UML Diagram
+---------------+
|  A (A-V, A-I, B-V, C-V)          |  B(B-V, B-I, C-V, D-V)            |
|  A(A-V, A-I, B-V, C-V)           |    A(A-V, A-I, B-V, C-V)              |
+---------------+
```
You can then add more plants by using the `@startuml` tag. For example:
```
# Plant UML Diagram
+---------------+
|  A (A-V, A-I, B-V, C-V)          |  B(B-V, B-I, C-V, D-V)            |
+---------------+
|    A(A-V, A-I, B-V, C-V)           |    D(D-V, D-I, B-V, E-V)             |
+---------------+
```
